claim.statement: Do not place consequential or destructive options immediately adjacent to, or styled the same as, benign frequent options. Proximity and visual sameness of high-consequence and low-consequence controls cause costly slips; separate, restyle, or relocate dangerous actions.

limitations: Layout trade-offs differ by surface; the principle is general, exact placement is contextual

do: Separate destructive actions from frequent benign ones with space or grouping
Give destructive actions distinct, recognisable styling
Consider relocating irreversible actions into an overflow or danger zone
avoid: Delete sitting flush against Save with identical appearance
Consequential menu items directly above/below routine ones with no separation

strategies: move irreversible actions to a danger zone
